root_text.an3.125:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati gotamake cetiye.
root_text.an3.125:2.2: Sanidānāhaṁ, bhikkhave, dhammaṁ desemi, no anidānaṁ.
root_text.an3.125:2.3: Sappāṭihāriyāhaṁ, bhikkhave, dhammaṁ desemi, no appāṭihāriyaṁ.
root_text.an3.125:2.4: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhave, abhiññāya dhammaṁ desayato no anabhiññāya, sanidānaṁ dhammaṁ desayato no anidānaṁ, sappāṭihāriyaṁ dhammaṁ desayato no appāṭihāriyaṁ, karaṇīyo ovādo, karaṇīyā anusāsanī.
root_text.an3.125:2.5: Alañca pana vo, bhikkhave, tuṭṭhiyā, alaṁ attamanatāya, alaṁ somanassāya:
root_text.an3.125:3.2: Attamanā te bhikkhū bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.
root_text.an3.125:3.3: Imasmiñca pana veyyākaraṇasmiṁ bhaññamāne sahassī lokadhātu akampitthāti.

translation_text.an3.125:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Gotamaka Shrine.
translation_text.an3.125:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an3.125:2.1: “Mendicants, I teach based on direct knowledge, not without direct knowledge.
translation_text.an3.125:2.2: I teach with reasons, not without them.
translation_text.an3.125:2.3: I teach with a demonstrable basis, not without it.
translation_text.an3.125:2.4: Since this is so, you should follow my advice and instruction.
translation_text.an3.125:2.5: This is enough for you to feel joyful, delighted, and happy:
translation_text.an3.125:2.6: ‘The Blessed One is a fully awakened Buddha! The teaching is well explained! The Saṅgha is practicing well!’”
translation_text.an3.125:3.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.an3.125:3.2: Satisfied, the mendicants approved what the Buddha said.
translation_text.an3.125:3.3: And while this discourse was being spoken, the thousandfold galaxy shook.
